name: Espresso Tuile
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the salt, brown sugar, and corn syrup in a mixer bowl with the paddle attachment.
2. Add the flour, butter, and vanilla extract to the mixture, then mix well.
3. Stir in the espresso beans and pecans.
4. Place heaping tablespoons of batter on a non-stick baking sheet (up to 3 cookies at a time).
5. Bake for 8-10 minutes in a preheated oven set to 350°F. Watch carefully.
6. Remove the cookie sheet from the oven and carefully peel off each tuile, one at a time, from the sheet.
7. Drape the cooled tuiles over three pieces of crumpled parchment paper. Timing is important; if the cookies become too hard before draping, place them back in the oven for a short period to soften. If they are too soft, they may tear when removed from the baking sheet.
